matches.moment: Haaland extends his 13-match scoring streak to pull Norway level just after the hour, but Brazil's superior chance quality produces a late go-ahead finish through Cunha that Norway's makeshift back line cannot repel a second time.

A Bellingham corner routine that has already produced goals this tournament finds Kane's head again in the 77th minute, canceling out a Quiñones counter-attack strike that punished England's legs after an hour at altitude.

verdict: Today's Round of 16 slate delivers one match with a clear data-backed favorite and one genuine coin-flip, and both readings are earned rather than defaulted. Brazil carry the sharper underlying numbers into MetLife Stadium, a clinical, low-volume attack posting their best-ever recorded xG-per-shot figure against a Norway side that has not kept a clean sheet in a fully-motivated match all tournament, but Erling Haaland's individual scoring streak is real enough to keep this from being a formality, and Brazil's own profile, needing a 95th-minute winner against Japan, shows this front line can go long stretches without finishing what it creates. In Mexico City, the data resists the temptation to hand this to England on reputation: Mexico's perfect defensive record, home altitude, and an extra day of rest stack up against an England side that has already shown it can be breached early and needed a second-half Kane rescue act of its own in the last round. Neither market nor public betting has fully resolved which way this one tips, and the numbers say they shouldn't have, this is the day's true 50/50. If Brazil advance as expected and Mexico or England survive extra time, the bracket's second week starts to take clearer shape around a Brazil side whose ceiling is obvious but whose floor, on the numbers, is not yet fully tested. — The Analyst

players.name: Vinícius Júnior
players.watch: Runs in behind Norway's high line; the primary beneficiary of Brazil's 0.16 xG/shot efficiency

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.stat: 5 tournament goals; scored in 13 straight competitive internationals (fallback stat)
players.watch: Direct physical duel with Gabriel; all 10 tournament shots have been first-time efforts

players.name: Julián Quiñones
players.stat: 3 goals, 1 assist - joint-most goal involvements at this World Cup
players.watch: Counter-attacking service against an England defense breached in 75% of its matches

players.name: Harry Kane
players.stat: 11 World Cup goals, England's all-time record (fallback stat)
players.watch: Set-piece positioning off Bellingham deliveries against Mexico's 100%-clean-sheet run
